cases.note: Fairy type does not exist in Gen 4. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/type_chart.c
Charizard is 4x weak to Rock; Stealth Rock deals 1/8 × 4 = 1/2 max HP. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_script.c StealthRockDamage
Blastoise is neutral to Rock; Stealth Rock deals 1/8 max HP. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_script.c StealthRockDamage

cases.source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_lib.c line 2489 — TYPE_ROCK, TYPE_STEEL, NOT_VERY_EFF
cases.note: Aggron (Steel/Rock) vs Rock: Steel resists Rock (0.5x, line 2489), Rock is neutral to Rock (1x, no entry). Combined: 0.5x. Stealth Rock deals 1/8 × 0.5 = 1/16. Note: prior value 0.03125 was incorrect (implied 0.25x which would require BOTH types to resist).
Dragonite (Dragon/Flying): Rock vs Dragon = 1x, Rock vs Flying = 2x. Combined 2x. 1/8 × 2 = 1/4 max HP.
Ninetales (Fire): Rock vs Fire = 2x. 1/8 × 2 = 1/4 max HP.
Articuno (Ice/Flying): Rock vs Ice = 2x, Rock vs Flying = 2x. Combined 4x. 1/8 × 4 = 1/2 max HP.
Gengar (Ghost/Poison): Rock vs Ghost = 1x, Rock vs Poison = 1x. Combined 1x neutral. 1/8 × 1 = 1/8 max HP.
Crunch became Physical in Gen 4 (was Special in Gen 1-3 via type-based split). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Flamethrower is Special in Gen 4 (Fire type; per-move category replaces per-type split). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Earthquake is Physical in Gen 4 (Ground type was always physical in type-split gens too). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Thunderbolt is Special in Gen 4. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Shadow Ball is Special in Gen 4 (Ghost type; was Special via type-split in Gen 1-3). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Dragon Claw became Physical in Gen 4 (was Special in Gen 3 via Dragon type-split).
Close Combat (introduced Gen 4) is a Physical Fighting move.
Aura Sphere (introduced Gen 4) is a Special Fighting move.
Trick Room has -7 priority (moves within Trick Room in reverse speed order). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Feint has +2 priority in Gen 4 (introduced in Gen 4). Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Aqua Jet (introduced Gen 4) has +1 priority.
Bullet Punch (introduced Gen 4) has +1 priority.
Vacuum Wave (introduced Gen 4) has +1 priority. Special Fighting priority move.
Sucker Punch (introduced Gen 4) has +1 priority.
Knock Off does NOT get 1.5x boost in Gen 4; that was introduced in Gen 6. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/data/battle/moves_info.c
Pursuit has base power 40, doubles to 80 when target switches out. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_script.c PursuitEffect
Close Combat base power = 120 in Gen 4. Unchanged in all later gens.
Aura Sphere base power = 90 in Gen 4. Reduced to 80 in Gen 6.
Iron Fist boosts punching moves by 1.2x. Introduced in Gen 4. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_script.c IronFist
Struggle recoil = floor(max_hp / 4) in Gen 3+. Source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_script.c StruggleEffect

cases.source: pret/pokeplatinum src/battle/battle_lib.c line 2493 — TYPE_GHOST, TYPE_STEEL, NOT_VERY_EFF
cases.note: Ghost-type moves are not very effective against Steel-type Pokemon in Gen 4 (0.5x). 'Steel resists Ghost' means Ghost→Steel=0.5x, NOT Steel→Ghost=0.5x. Steel attacking Ghost has no type chart entry = 1x neutral.
Water attacking Steel is neutral (1x) in Gen 4. The Steel-resists-Water entry was not added until Gen 4 type chart revision? Actually Gen 4 kept Water vs Steel = 1x neutral same as Gen 2-3.
Electric attacking Steel is neutral (1x) in Gen 4. Same as Gen 2-3.
Endure has +3 priority in Gen 4 per pokeplatinum. @pkmn/data reports +4 (Showdown approximation). Pret wins.
